lede: Launched in 2014 to address the shortage of church organists in Surrey, the SOA Organ Student Scheme offers six free-of-charge lessons to pianists or keyboard players, giving them a chance to try the organ before committing further.

sections.title: Who it's for
sections.body: There is no age restriction — participants have ranged from teenagers to retirees. The scheme is best suited to those with a reasonable level of keyboard experience, typically ABRSM Grade 5 or above.

sections.title: After the initial lessons
sections.body: Students who wish to continue can pursue further organ training in a number of ways — supported by their church, through personal funds, or with the help of other grants. Several Surrey churches now employ or rotate graduates of the scheme.

sections.title: How to apply
sections.body: For more information and to apply, please contact us at [enquiries@surreyorganistsassociation.org](mailto:enquiries@surreyorganistsassociation.org).
